titleStmt.title: Letter from William M. Nolan to Matthew Nathan, 31 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William M. Nolan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from the Town Clerk of Limerick William M. Nolan (1855-1941) to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) the under-secretary for Ireland. Nathan was under-secretary until the 1916 Rising after which he offered his resignation. The letter deals with the resignation of a Mr Reidy and particularly the amount of an allowance he will receive. The letter states that the council are considering a sum of £94:0:8 per year.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5711

correspAction.placeName: Town Clerk's Office, Town Hall, Limerick, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Town Clerk's Office,
Town Hall,
Limerick. 31st March, 1916.
4810. re Superranuation of Mr. Patrick Reidy.
Sir, I am directed by the Limerick County Borough Council to state
in reply to your letter of the 20th instant herein, that they
desire to award a retiring allowance of £94:0:8 per annum to Mr.
Reidy. This figure represents 31/60ths of the salary of £3 per
week, which he enjoyed, as general Foreman of the Gas Works, from
1897 to 1913, and of his emoluments ( " house, coal, and light" valued
at Ten shillings per week. There will be a meeting of my Council on Thursday next , the
6th prox. , and it would facilitate matters if I was in a position to
inform the Council that their proposal to grant an allowance of
£94:0:8 per annum to Mr. Reidy had the sanction of His Excellency ,
The Lord Lieutenant. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, W. M. Nolan
Town Clerk. The Under Secretary,
Dublin Castle.
